title: The Elderly Neighbor Who Became My Second Grandmother

all.en: When my husband and I moved into the old apartment on Renai Road, our neighbor was already eighty-two.
all.zh: 當我先生和我搬進仁愛路那棟老公寓時，鄰居已經八十二歲了。

all.en: Her name was Mrs. Chen, and she lived alone in the apartment directly across the narrow hallway.
all.zh: 她姓陳，獨自一人住在那條狹窄走廊正對面的公寓裡。

all.en: Her only son had immigrated to Canada more than fifteen years ago with his Canadian wife.
all.zh: 她唯一的兒子十五年多以前就和他的加拿大太太移民到加拿大去了。

all.en: On the day we moved in, she knocked on our door holding a small plate of warm pineapple cakes.
all.zh: 我們搬進來那天，她端著一小盤溫熱的鳳梨酥來敲我們的門。

all.en: Her eyes were sharp behind her thick glasses, and her smile was warmer than the afternoon sun.
all.zh: 她那雙厚厚眼鏡後面的眼睛炯炯有神，笑容比午後的陽光還要溫暖。

all.en: She asked us where we were from and listened carefully as though she had nothing more important to do.
all.zh: 她問我們從哪裡來，仔細地聆聽，彷彿她沒有任何更重要的事要做。

all.en: From that first conversation, my husband and I knew she was someone special in our new neighborhood.
all.zh: 從那第一次的對話開始，我先生和我就知道她是這個新社區裡很特別的人。

all.en: Every Wednesday morning, I started inviting her over for tea before I left for the office.
all.zh: 每個星期三早上，在我去上班之前，我都會邀她過來喝杯茶。

all.en: She always brought something with her, whether it was rice balls, fresh fruit, or her own pickled radish.
all.zh: 她每次都會帶點東西過來，有時是飯糰，有時是新鮮水果，或是她自己醃的蘿蔔。

all.en: She told stories about old Taipei from a time when our street was still lined with rice fields.
all.zh: 她講述著昔日台北的故事，那時我們這條街兩旁還是稻田。

all.en: I learned that her husband had been a postman who passed away from a sudden heart attack in 1998.
all.zh: 我得知她的先生曾是一位郵差，於一九九八年因突發心臟病過世。

all.en: She had never remarried because she said one good love story was already enough for one lifetime.
all.zh: 她從未再婚，她說一輩子有過一段美好的愛情故事就已經足夠了。

all.en: One winter morning, I heard a loud thud from across the hall and rushed over in my pajamas.
all.zh: 某個冬天的早晨，我聽到走廊對面傳來重重的一聲，我穿著睡衣就衝了過去。

all.en: She had slipped on a wet bathroom tile and could not get up by herself from the floor.
all.zh: 她在浴室濕滑的磁磚上摔了一跤，自己沒辦法從地上爬起來。

all.en: I called an ambulance and held her hand until the paramedics gently lifted her onto a stretcher.
all.zh: 我叫了救護車，並一直握著她的手，直到救護人員輕輕地把她抬上擔架。

all.en: Her hip was bruised but not broken, and she returned home after only two nights in the hospital.
all.zh: 她的髖部瘀青但沒有骨折，在醫院住了僅僅兩晚之後就回家了。

all.en: When her son called from Canada that night, she put me on the phone to thank me directly.
all.zh: 那天晚上她兒子從加拿大打電話來時，她把電話遞給我讓他親自向我道謝。

all.en: He cried on the phone and asked if I could keep an eye on his mother from now on.
all.zh: 他在電話中哭了，問我能不能從現在開始幫他多看顧母親。

all.en: I promised him I would, but the truth was, she had already become like family to me.
all.zh: 我答應了他，但事實上她早就已經像我的家人一樣了。

all.en: After that, my husband installed grab bars in her bathroom and a brighter light above the entrance.
all.zh: 從那之後，我先生在她的浴室裝了扶手，並在門口上方換了更亮的燈。

all.en: She insisted on paying us back with handmade dumplings on the last Sunday of every month.
all.zh: 她堅持每個月的最後一個星期天用手工水餃來回報我們。

all.en: When I became pregnant the following autumn, she was the first person we told outside our parents.
all.zh: 隔年秋天我懷孕時，她是父母以外我們第一個告知的人。

all.en: Her eyes filled with tears, and she said she had been waiting for a baby in this building for years.
all.zh: 她的眼眶頓時泛淚，她說她在這棟樓裡已經盼著有個寶寶好多年了。

all.en: Throughout my pregnancy, she knitted a soft yellow blanket and tiny matching booties for the baby.
all.zh: 整個懷孕期間，她為寶寶織了一條柔軟的黃色毯子和一雙搭配的小襪套。

all.en: She also shared old folk remedies for morning sickness that actually worked better than medicine.
all.zh: 她還分享了一些治療害喜的古老偏方，效果竟然比藥還要好。

all.en: When our son was born in March, she came to the hospital with a single white orchid in a pot.
all.zh: 三月我兒子出生時，她帶著一盆只有一朵的白色蘭花來到醫院。

all.en: She held him as carefully as if he were the only baby ever born in human history.
all.zh: 她抱著他的小心模樣，彷彿他是人類史上唯一出生過的嬰兒。

all.en: Our son grew up calling her Ah-ma, the Taiwanese word he used for both grandmothers.
all.zh: 我們的兒子從小就叫她阿嬤，這也是他叫兩位奶奶的台語稱呼。

all.en: She taught him traditional songs my husband and I had never even heard before in our own childhoods.
all.zh: 她教他唱我先生和我在自己童年時都從沒聽過的傳統歌謠。

all.en: On her ninetieth birthday, we threw a small party in her apartment with her favorite red bean soup.
all.zh: 在她九十歲生日那天，我們在她的公寓辦了一個小派對，準備了她最愛的紅豆湯。

all.en: Her son flew in from Canada as a complete surprise, and she cried for almost ten minutes straight.
all.zh: 她兒子從加拿大飛回來給她一個大驚喜，她哭了將近整整十分鐘。

all.en: When she passed away peacefully in her sleep two years later, our son lost his second grandmother.
all.zh: 兩年後當她在睡夢中安詳離世時，我們的兒子失去了他的第二個奶奶。

all.en: He insisted on wearing the yellow booties she had knitted him to her quiet little funeral.
all.zh: 他堅持要穿著她為他織的黃色小襪套去參加她那場安靜的小型告別式。

all.en: Her son gave us a small wooden box she had labeled clearly with our family's name on top.
all.zh: 她的兒子把一個小木盒交給我們，上面清楚地寫著我們家的名字。

all.en: Inside was a handwritten note that simply read, thank you for letting me belong to your family.
all.zh: 盒子裡有一張親筆寫的紙條，上面只寫著：謝謝你們讓我也成為你們家的一份子。

all.en: Beneath the note lay a small jade pendant carved into the shape of a sleeping child.
all.zh: 紙條下面躺著一塊小巧的玉墜，雕刻成熟睡孩子的模樣。

all.en: My husband fastened it around our son's neck on the morning of his first day of school.
all.zh: 我先生在兒子上小學第一天的早上，把它繫在兒子的脖子上。

all.en: Our son still rubs the smooth jade whenever he feels nervous before any new beginning.
all.zh: 每當我們的兒子在面對新開始而感到緊張時，仍會撫摸那塊光滑的玉墜。

all.en: We turned her empty apartment into a quiet reading room for the children of our building.
all.zh: 我們把她空出來的公寓改造成本棟樓孩子們的安靜閱讀室。

all.en: Her son agreed without hesitation and donated all of her old books to fill the shelves.
all.zh: 她兒子毫不猶豫地同意了，並捐出她所有的舊書填滿了書架。

all.en: Every Saturday afternoon, neighborhood children gather there to read and sip warm barley tea.
all.zh: 每個週六下午，社區的孩子們聚集在那裡看書，啜飲溫熱的麥茶。

all.en: I sometimes volunteer to read picture books aloud, just the way she used to read to my son.
all.zh: 我有時會自願大聲朗讀繪本，就像她以前對我兒子做的那樣。

all.en: On the anniversary of her passing, we always cook her recipe of dumplings as a family.
all.zh: 在她逝世週年那天，我們全家一定會一起煮她的水餃食譜。

all.en: Our son carefully folds the pleats now, claiming his Ah-ma taught him through his fingers.
all.zh: 兒子如今能細心地捏出褶子，他說阿嬤透過他的手指頭教會了他。

all.en: We hope someday he will pass these warm Wednesday teas on to a neighbor of his own.
all.zh: 我們盼望有一天他也會把這溫暖的星期三茶會傳承給他自己的鄰居。

all.en: Years later, our son brought his first university girlfriend home to introduce her to the family.
all.zh: 多年之後，我們的兒子帶他大學時期的第一任女朋友回家，向家人介紹她。

all.en: She noticed the jade pendant immediately and asked about its quiet story with genuine curiosity.
all.zh: 她立刻注意到那塊玉墜，並帶著真誠的好奇心詢問它背後安靜的故事。

all.en: Our son told her about Mrs. Chen for nearly an hour, his eyes shining brightly the whole time.
all.zh: 兒子花了將近一小時跟她說陳奶奶的故事，眼睛從頭到尾都閃閃發亮。

all.en: She listened carefully and asked if she could one day meet the children in the reading room.
all.zh: 她仔細聆聽，並問她是否有一天能去閱讀室見見那裡的孩子們。

all.en: The following Saturday, she came to volunteer and read picture books aloud to a circle of children.
all.zh: 接下來的週六，她來當志工，為一圈孩子們大聲朗讀繪本。

all.en: Watching her gentle patience reminded me deeply of Mrs. Chen's warm presence in the old apartment.
all.zh: 看著她溫柔的耐心，讓我深深想起陳奶奶在老公寓裡溫暖的身影。

all.en: I quietly hoped she might someday become part of our family in a more permanent way.
all.zh: 我悄悄地希望，她有一天能以更長久的方式成為我們家的一份子。

all.en: Two years later, she and our son married in a small garden ceremony beneath a flowering tree.
all.zh: 兩年後，她和我們的兒子在一棵開花的樹下舉行了一場小型花園婚禮。

all.en: Our son carried the jade pendant in his pocket and rubbed it before saying his vows aloud.
all.zh: 兒子把那塊玉墜放在口袋裡，在大聲說出誓詞前撫摸了它。

all.en: I imagined Mrs. Chen smiling from somewhere, finally complete in the family she had quietly joined.
all.zh: 我想像著陳奶奶在某處微笑著，終於在她悄悄加入的家庭中得到了圓滿。

all.en: Our son's new wife took over running the reading room and added a small weekend storytelling hour.
all.zh: 我們兒子的新婚妻子接手了閱讀室的經營，並新增了一個小型的週末說故事時段。

all.en: Children from three nearby apartment buildings now gather there every Saturday for picture books and snacks.
all.zh: 現在每個星期六，附近三棟公寓的孩子們都聚在那裡看繪本、吃點心。

all.en: Mrs. Chen's son flew back from Canada to attend the second anniversary party of the small reading room.
all.zh: 陳奶奶的兒子從加拿大飛回來參加那間小閱讀室的兩週年派對。

all.en: He donated his late mother's old wooden chairs to give the children proper seating around the tables.
all.zh: 他捐出了已故母親的老木椅，讓孩子們有合適的座位圍著桌子坐。

all.en: Our son and his wife eventually had their own baby and named her after Mrs. Chen's late mother.
all.zh: 我們的兒子和他的妻子後來生了自己的寶寶，並用陳奶奶過世母親的名字為她取名。

all.en: The baby's first lullaby was one of the traditional songs Mrs. Chen had taught my son decades ago.
all.zh: 寶寶聽的第一首搖籃曲，正是陳奶奶幾十年前教我兒子的傳統歌謠之一。

all.en: I taught my granddaughter to fold pleated dumplings using the same technique Mrs. Chen showed me first.
all.zh: 我用陳奶奶當初教我的同樣手法，教孫女捏出有褶子的水餃。

all.en: My granddaughter wears the jade pendant on her own first day of kindergarten with shining proud eyes.
all.zh: 我的孫女在她自己幼兒園的第一天，戴著那塊玉墜，眼睛閃耀著驕傲的光芒。

all.en: She still rubs it before any new beginning, just as her father had done years before her.
all.zh: 她在面對任何新開始之前仍會撫摸它，就像多年前她父親所做的那樣。

all.en: Sometimes I sit alone in the reading room at dusk and quietly thank Mrs. Chen for everything still here.
all.zh: 有時黃昏時分我獨自坐在閱讀室裡，靜靜地感謝陳奶奶留下的一切。
